summary: A lo‑fi audience cassette that’s only partially audible, but it captures a fiery Cryptical → The Other One → Cosmic Charlie stretch and enthusiastic vocals from Jerry and Bobby.
review: The recording is an imperfect, tape‑sourced capture – muffled at times, missing much of the drum mix and incomplete beyond the first half of the set – yet fans can hear a powerful early‑’70 vibe. The highlight is the intense Cryptical/Other One/Cosmic Charlie sequence with Jerry Garcia and Bob Weir delivering impassioned vocals, even if the overall sound quality limits repeat listening.

ai_rating.rationale: Show performance is praised for standout jams and vocal energy (≈70% weight), but recording quality is consistently described as poor, incomplete, or only listenable at high volume (≈30% weight).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tape‑sourced, distorted and muffled; drums are thin or missing; the tape is incomplete, ending after the Cosmic Charlie segment. Listeners report it’s only tolerable at high volume.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set list is fragmentary in the recording, but the early jam sequence (Cryptical → The Other One → Cosmic Charlie) is highlighted as a high‑energy peak.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His singing is noted as heartfelt and driving during Cryptical.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as “very high‑strung” on Cryptical; strong vocal presence throughout the highlight stretch.
band_member_comments.Drums: Commenters note a lack of drum presence or thin drum mix.
